rulings.Case Title: ANNE LYNCH ET AL VS. ENVIVIO, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition By Anne Lynch And Erik Miller To Confirm Final Arbitration Decision
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 29, 2018, Line 1, PETITIONER ANNE LYNCH, ERIK MILLER's Hearing On Petition By Anne Lynch And Erik Miller To Confirm Final Arbitration Decision. Petitioners Anne Lynch and Erik Miller's petition to confirm final arbitration award is dismissed. The petition is moot because respondents Envivio, Inc. and Ericsson, Inc. paid the full amount required by the final award. The California Arbitration Act empowers a court to dismiss a petition to confirm when an award has been satisfied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Respondents is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: CRAIG A HOSKINSON VS. YUANDA CHINA HOLDINGS, LTD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion To Enforce Settlement Pursuant To Stipulation For Settlement And For Entry Of Judgment Thereon
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 29, 2018, Line 12, Plaintiff'S Motion To Enforce Settlement Pursuant To Stipulation For Settlement And For Entry Of Judgment Thereon. Hearing required. Plaintiff Craig Hoskinson's motion to enforce settlement is denied. The sole procedural ground for the motion is CCP section 664.6, but that section provides for summary enforcement of a settlement only where there has been an oral stipulation before the court or a written agreement signed by all parties to the agreement. Mr. Hoskinson has failed to provide evidence of either an oral stipulation before the court or a written agreement signed by all parties to the agreement. The court is inclined to set a new trial date in the very near future and in the interim require the parties to meet with Pang Ly, the court's settlement attorney, as soon as possible. The court will also entertain an application for an order shortening time to move to amend the complaint to add a claim for breach of settlement agreement.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: JOSE MANUEL CRUZ-QUINTANA ET AL VS. PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Central Moloney, Incs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 29, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANT CENTRAL MOLONEY, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT's Defendant Central Moloney, Incs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment. Defendant Central Moloney, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. The declaration of Glen Stevick creates triable disputes whether the injuries to plaintiff Jose Cruz-Quintana were caused by the defective design of the transformer and whether the transformer was state of the art at the time it was manufactured. Central Moloney's objections to Mr. Stevick's declaration are overruled. The objections go to the weight, not the admissibility, of Mr. Stevick's opinions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: REGIONAL PROTECTION AGENCY, INC. VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 29, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.'s motion to strike the punitive damages allegations and prayers from the second and third causes of action for breach of the implied covenant and interference with contractual relations in the first amended complaint is granted without prejudice to plaintiff Regional Protection Agency, Inc. filing a motion to amend to seek punitive damages as to the interference claim if and when it obtains facts allowing it to do so. An implied covenant claim is a contract claim for which punitive damages are not available. RPA has not alleged sufficient ultimate facts supporting corporate liability for punitive damages against Uber on its interference claim nor does it appear that it is now able to do so. However, after discovery and factual investigation, it may be able to do so and is not precluded from filing a motion to amend to do so if it obtains sufficient supporting facts. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Uber is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)
